title: Programs
subtitle: Structured pathways to contribute, learn, partner, mentor, fund, and steward open work.
audience: Potential volunteers, interns, fellows, mentors, partners, funders, and open-source contributors.
lede: Institute Programs turn interest into participation through volunteer, internship, mentorship, fellowship, partnership, philanthropy, grants, and open-source pathways. All programs are open to people of any background and familiarity level.

sections.heading: Volunteer
sections.body: Volunteering is the broadest on-ramp for participation. Volunteers contribute to learning groups, projects, operations, writing, analysis, media production, and community support according to available time, background, and skills. No prior expertise in Active Inference is required — many volunteers begin by attending meetings, watching recordings, and contributing in whatever way fits their current capacity.

sections.heading: Internship
sections.body: Internships provide structured engagement for participants who want to contribute more deeply to specific projects or functions. Interns work on defined deliverables — documentation, analysis, code, educational materials, or operational support — and develop skills through project practice and community engagement. Learning is built into the process regardless of prior experience.

sections.heading: Mentorship
sections.body: Mentorship connects experienced participants with people earlier in their engagement with Active Inference or the Institute. Mentors support the development of mentees through guidance, feedback, shared project work, and professional connection. Mentorship can be formal — with defined goals and regular check-ins — or informal, emerging naturally from community participation.

sections.heading: Fellowship
sections.body: The Fellowship pathway supports contributors who provide regular progress reports and develop research, applications, or public resources aligned with the Institute and Ecosystem. Fellows make sustained commitments to specific projects or areas of work, share updates with the community on a regular schedule, and produce public outputs — papers, software, educational materials, or project deliverables.

sections.heading: Grants
sections.body: The Institute supports grant proposals and funding efforts aligned with Active Inference research, education, and application. Participants and projects can seek Institute support for grant preparation, institutional affiliation, and collaborative funding proposals. The Institute tracks funding opportunities and shares them through its regular communications.

sections.heading: Partnership
sections.body: Partnerships align organizations, research groups, funders, and collaborators around concrete shared work. Partner organizations may co-host events, co-author publications, share resources, provide infrastructure, or collaborate on research and education projects. Partnership is a practical exchange of time, attention, expertise, funding, and institutional support.

sections.heading: Philanthropy
sections.body: As a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit, the Active Inference Institute accepts tax-deductible donations from individuals and organizations. Philanthropic support funds public education, research infrastructure, events, community tools, and ecosystem stewardship.

sections.heading: Open Source
sections.body: Open-source contribution is an ecosystem support function: publish reusable work, improve project infrastructure, and make shared technical resources discoverable and maintainable. The Institute hosts and contributes to open-source repositories for Active Inference implementations, tools, datasets, ontologies, and educational materials.

cards.title: Volunteer
cards.text: Contribute to learning groups, project support, operations, writing, analysis, or media.

cards.title: Internship
cards.text: Structured contribution pathway with learning, documentation, and project practice.

cards.title: Mentorship
cards.text: Support participant development, or find a mentor to guide your own engagement.

cards.title: Fellowship
cards.text: Develop research or applied work with regular reporting and public outputs.

cards.title: Grants
cards.text: Institute support for grant preparation, affiliation, and collaborative funding proposals.

cards.title: Partnership
cards.text: Align organizations and collaborators around concrete shared research or education work.

cards.title: Philanthropy
cards.text: Support public education, research infrastructure, and ecosystem stewardship as a 501(c)(3) donor.

cards.title: Open Source
cards.text: Publish reusable work, improve project infrastructure, and contribute to shared Active Inference tools.
